variable {M' : Type*} [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M'] (f : M →ₗ[R] M') [IsLocalizedModule S f]
variable {N' : Type*} [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] (g : N →ₗ[R] N') [IsLocalizedModule S g]

/--
Let `M` be a finite `R`-module, and `N` be a finitely presented `R`-module.
If `l : M →ₗ[R] N` is a linear map whose localization at `S : Submonoid R` is bijective,
then `l` is already bijective under the localization at some `r ∈ S`.
-/
lemma exists_bijective_map_powers [Module.Finite R M] [Module.FinitePresentation R N]
    (l : M →ₗ[R] N) (hf : Function.Bijective (IsLocalizedModule.map S f g l)) :
    ∃ r, r ∈ S ∧ ∀ t, r ∣ t → Function.Bijective (LocalizedModule.map (.powers t) l) := by
  let e : M' ≃ₗ[R] N' := LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ hf
  obtain ⟨l', s₀, H⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_lift_of_isLocalizedModule S f
    (e.symm.toLinearMap.comp g)
  have H₁ : g ∘ₗ l ∘ₗ l' = g ∘ₗ (s₀ • LinearMap.id) := by
    ext a; simpa [-EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq, e] using congr(e ($H a))
  obtain ⟨s₁, hs₁⟩ := Module.Finite.exists_smul_of_comp_eq_of_isLocalizedModule S g _ _ H₁
  have H₂ : f ∘ₗ l' ∘ₗ l = f ∘ₗ (s₀ • LinearMap.id) := by
    rw [← LinearMap.comp_assoc, H, LinearMap.smul_comp, LinearMap.comp_assoc,
      ← IsLocalizedModule.map_comp S f g l, ← LinearMap.comp_assoc]
    change s₀ • (e.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap) ∘ₗ _ = _
    simp [LinearMap.comp_smul]
  obtain ⟨s₂, hs₂⟩ := Module.Finite.exists_smul_of_comp_eq_of_isLocalizedModule S f _ _ H₂
  refine ⟨s₀ * s₁ * s₂, (s₀ * s₁ * s₂).2, fun t ht ↦ ?_⟩
  let Rₛ := Localization (.powers t)
  let lₛ := LocalizedModule.map (.powers t) l
  have hu := IsLocalization.map_units (M := .powers t) Rₛ ⟨t, Submonoid.mem_powers t⟩
  have hu₀ : IsUnit (algebraMap R Rₛ s₀) := isUnit_of_dvd_unit
      (map_dvd (algebraMap R Rₛ) (dvd_trans ⟨s₁ * s₂, by simp [mul_assoc]⟩ ht)) hu
  have hu₁ : IsUnit (algebraMap R Rₛ s₁) := isUnit_of_dvd_unit
      (map_dvd (algebraMap R Rₛ) (dvd_trans ⟨s₀ * s₂, by ring⟩ ht)) hu
  have hu₂ : IsUnit (algebraMap R Rₛ s₂) := isUnit_of_dvd_unit
      (map_dvd (algebraMap R Rₛ) (dvd_trans ⟨s₀ * s₁, by ring⟩ ht)) hu
  let lₛ' := LocalizedModule.map (.powers t) l'
  have H_left : ((hu₀.unit⁻¹).1 • lₛ') ∘ₗ lₛ = LinearMap.id := by
    apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp (hu₂.map (algebraMap Rₛ (Module.End Rₛ _)))).1
    apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp (hu₀.map (algebraMap Rₛ (Module.End Rₛ _)))).1
    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, algebraMap_smul, LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower]
    rw [LinearMap.smul_comp, ← smul_assoc s₀.1, Algebra.smul_def s₀.1, IsUnit.mul_val_inv, one_smul]
    apply LinearMap.restrictScalars_injective R
    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers t) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (.powers t) M)
      (IsLocalizedModule.map_units (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (.powers t) M))
    ext x
    have : s₂.1 • l' (l x) = s₂.1 • s₀.1 • x := congr($hs₂ x)
    simp [lₛ, lₛ', LocalizedModule.smul'_mk, this]
  have H_right : lₛ ∘ₗ ((hu₀.unit⁻¹).1 • lₛ') = LinearMap.id := by
    apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp (hu₁.map (algebraMap Rₛ (Module.End Rₛ _)))).1
    apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp (hu₀.map (algebraMap Rₛ (Module.End Rₛ _)))).1
    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, algebraMap_smul, LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower]
    rw [LinearMap.comp_smul, ← smul_assoc s₀.1, Algebra.smul_def s₀.1, IsUnit.mul_val_inv, one_smul]
    apply LinearMap.restrictScalars_injective R
    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers t) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (.powers t) N)
      (IsLocalizedModule.map_units (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (.powers t) N))
    ext x
    have : s₁.1 • l (l' x) = s₁.1 • s₀.1 • x := congr($hs₁ x)
    simp [lₛ, lₛ', LocalizedModule.smul'_mk, this]
  let eₛ : LocalizedModule.Away t M ≃ₗ[Rₛ] LocalizedModule.Away t N :=
    { __ := lₛ,
      invFun := ((hu₀.unit⁻¹).1 • lₛ'),
      left_inv := fun x ↦ congr($H_left x),
      right_inv := fun x ↦ congr($H_right x) }

lemma Module.FinitePresentation.exists_notMem_bijective [Module.Finite R M]
    [Module.FinitePresentation R N] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] {Mₚ Nₚ : Type*}

    (fM : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) (fN : N →ₗ[R] Nₚ)

    ∃ (g : R), g ∉ p ∧ Function.Bijective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers g) f) := by
  obtain ⟨g, hg, h⟩ := exists_bijective_map_powers p.primeCompl fM fN f hf
  exact ⟨g, hg, h g dvd_rfl⟩

Source file (extracted LaTeX, authoritative for line anchors): `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex` (single-file arXiv e-print math/0504590, 3390 lines; ASCII). ## 1. Precise statement and location

The n=0 case is NOT a separately numbered lemma. It is the paragraph "**Special case:** Let $n=0$, so that $\PP^n_S=S$" inside the proof of **Theorem 4.3** (LaTeX `\label{flattening stratification}`), Section 4 "Generic Flatness and Flattening Stratification" (`\section` at L1698; theorem statement L1811–1841; n=0 proof L1849–1885). Theorem numbering verified from the source counter (`\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]`; the only prior numbered items in §4 are Lemma 4.1 = `algebraic lemma for generic flatness` L1711 and Theorem 4.2 = `generic flatness` L1781).

Theorem 4.3 (full statement, L1811–1841): S noetherian, F coherent on P^n_S. Then (a) the set I of Hilbert polynomials of restrictions of F to fibres of P^n_S → S is finite; (b) for each f ∈ I there is a locally closed subscheme S_f ⊂ S with:
- **(i) Point-set** (L1820–1824): |S_f| = {s ∈ S : Hilbert polynomial of F|_{P^n_s} is f}; the |S_f| are disjoint with union |S|.
- **(ii) Universal property** (L1826–1834): with S' = ∐_f S_f and i: S' → S induced by the inclusions, i*F on P^n_{S'} is flat over S'; and for any φ: T → S, φ*F on P^n_T is flat over T **iff** φ factors through i: S' → S. S_f is uniquely determined by f.
- **(iii) Closure of strata** (L1836–1840): order I by f < g iff f(n) < g(n) for n ≫ 0; then closure of |S_f| ⊆ ∪_{f ≤ g} |S_g|.

**Specialization to n=0** (as Nitsure sets it up, L1849–1857): P^0_S = S, F coherent on S itself. The "fiber" F|_s is the pullback of F to Spec κ(s), i.e. F_s ⊗_{O_{S,s}} κ(s) (he says this explicitly, L1852–1854). The Hilbert polynomial of the fibre at s is the degree-0 (constant) polynomial e = dim_{κ(s)} F|_s (L1855–1857). So the n=0 statement is: **for coherent F on noetherian S there are finitely many disjoint locally closed strata S_e ⊂ S covering S, |S_e| = {s : dim_{κ(s)} F|_s = e}, such that for any f: T → S, f*F is locally free of rank e iff f factors through S_e; and closure(|S_e|) ⊆ ∪_{e' ≥ e} |S_{e'}|.** (The "flat" form of (ii) becomes: f*F flat over T iff f factors through ∐_e S_e.)

## 2. Proof structure, step by step (verbatim-faithful to L1844–1885)

**Step 0 — gluing reduction (stated once for the whole theorem, L1844–1846):** "It is enough to prove the theorem for open subschemes of S which cover S, as the resulting strata will then glue together by their universal property." I.e. the construction below is local on S; on an overlap V ∩ V′, the two candidate strata represent the same subfunctor of Hom(−, V ∩ V′) ("f*F locally free of rank e"), and a subscheme is determined by the subfunctor it represents (this is also the source of the uniqueness claim in (ii)). Noetherian ⇒ quasicompact ⇒ finitely many opens suffice.

**Step 1 — Nakayama prolongation to a local presentation (L1859–1864):** Fix s ∈ S, let e = dim_{κ(s)} F|_s. By Nakayama, any κ(s)-basis of F|_s prolongs to a neighbourhood U of s giving generators of F|_U (surjection O_U^e → F|_U). "Repeating this argument" (i.e. applying the same generation argument to the kernel, which is coherent since S is noetherian) gives a smaller neighbourhood V ∋ s and a right-exact sequence
  O_V^⊕m --ψ--> O_V^⊕e --φ--> F → 0.
Note the middle rank is exactly e = fibre dimension at s, so this is a minimal presentation at s: ψ ⊗ κ(s) = 0, i.e. all entries of ψ lie in m_s; hence s itself will lie in the stratum below.

**Step 2 — the entry-ideal closed subscheme V_e (L1865–1869):** Let I_e ⊂ O_V be the ideal sheaf generated by the e×m matrix entries ψ_{i,j} of ψ. Let V_e ⊂ V be the closed subscheme defined by I_e.

**Step 3 — universal property of V_e (L1870–1878):** For any morphism f: T → V, the pulled-back sequence O_T^m --f*ψ--> O_T^e --f*φ--> f*F → 0 is exact "by right-exactness of tensor products". Hence **f*F is locally free of rank e iff f*ψ = 0 iff f factors through V_e ↪ V** (f*ψ = 0 ⟺ every entry ψ_{i,j} pulls back to 0 ⟺ f*(I_e)·O_T = 0 ⟺ f factors through V(I_e)). Nitsure states the first "iff" without proof; the two directions are: (⇐) f*ψ = 0 gives f*F ≅ O_T^e; (⇒, the direction needing an argument in a formalization) if f*F is locally free of rank e then the surjection O_T^e ↠ f*F between locally frees of the same rank e is an isomorphism (fibrewise: a surjection of e-dimensional vector spaces is injective; globalize by Nakayama, or use the determinant trick), so its kernel im(f*ψ) = 0. "Thus we have proved assertions (i) and (ii) of the theorem" (L1879). Point-set (i) follows from (ii) applied to T = Spec κ(s′) → V: F|_{s′} is locally free of rank e over κ(s′) iff dim_{κ(s′)} F|_{s′} = e, so |V_e| = {s′ ∈ V : fibre rank = e}; this simultaneously gives disjointness (a κ(s′)-vector space has one dimension) and covering (every point has some fibre rank, and lies in the V built around it, in that V's rank-e stratum by Step 1's minimality).

**Step 4 — semicontinuity ⇒ (iii) (L1882–1885):** "As the rank of the matrix (ψ_{i,j}) is lower semi-continuous, it follows that the function e is upper semi-continuous, which proves the assertion (iii)". Mechanism: on V, exactness of the fibre sequence gives dim F|_{s′} = e − rank(ψ(s′)); rank of a matrix of functions is lower semicontinuous (some r×r minor nonvanishing is an open condition), so s′ ↦ dim F|_{s′} is upper semicontinuous, which is exactly (iii) for constant polynomials (closure of the rank-e locus meets only rank ≥ e loci). That completes the n=0 case in the text.

**Finiteness of the strata (not re-argued in the n=0 paragraph):** finiteness of I is part of the theorem's assertion; for n=0 it follows from the construction: on each local V every fibre rank is ≤ e (the presentation bounds it), noetherian S is covered by finitely many such V, so the rank function is bounded and only finitely many S_e are nonempty. (In the general-n part Nitsure derives finiteness (statement **(A)**, L1927–1929) from generic flatness + noetherian induction, L1899–1922.)

**Universal property of the total stratification ∐_e S_e (flat version of (ii)):** if φ*F is flat over T then, φ*F being coherent over locally noetherian T, it is locally free, so its rank is locally constant; T decomposes as a disjoint union of open-and-closed pieces T_e of constant rank e; each T_e → S factors through S_e by Step 3 + gluing; hence φ factors through ∐ S_e. Conversely i*F is locally free (rank e on S_e), hence flat. Nitsure spells out this "decompose T by locally constant Hilbert polynomial into open-closed T_f" argument only in the general case (L2074–2081), and it applies verbatim for n=0.

**How the general case consumes the n=0 case (context, L1888–2090):** set E_i = π_*F(N+i), i = 0..n (L1992–1994); apply the n=0 case to E_0 to get strata (W_{e_0}) with the "locally free of rank e_0 iff factors" property (L1995–2000); iterate on each stratum for E_1, ..., E_n giving W_{e_0,…,e_n} in n+1 steps (L2001–2009); reindex tuples by numerical polynomials of degree ≤ n via e_i = f(N+i) (L2013–2021); then refine W_f to a closed subscheme S_f ⊂ W_f with |S_f| = |W_f| by imposing local freeness of π_*F(N+i) for ALL i ≥ 0 — each gives an ideal I_i, and the increasing chain I_0 ⊂ I_0+I_1 ⊂ … stabilizes by noetherianness, so I = ΣI_i is coherent and S_f = V(I) (L2037–2058). Flat over S_f then follows from Lemma 3.2 (`graded module is flat`, L1403–1421: if π_*F(r) is locally free for all r ≥ N then F is flat over S) plus Lemma 3.1 (`base change without flatness`, L1278) (L2065–2073). Property (iii) in general is reduced back to the n=0 semicontinuity applied to the single sheaf π_*F(p) for one large twist p (L2083–2088).

## 3. Subtle points (for the formalization)

- **Locally closed, not closed:** V_e is closed only inside the open V on which the e-generator presentation exists; globally S_e = (closed subscheme of an open) = locally closed. It genuinely cannot be closed: e.g. F = coker(O --x--> O) on A^1 has S_1 = {0} (closed) and S_0 = A^1∖{0} (open, not closed). The rank-e locus is "open in its closure" precisely because of upper semicontinuity: {rank ≤ e} is open... more precisely {rank ≥ e+1} is closed and {rank ≥ e} is closed, and |S_e| = {rank ≥ e} ∖ {rank ≥ e+1}.
- **Direction of the semicontinuities:** matrix rank of ψ is LOWER semicontinuous ⇒ fibre dimension e(s) = e − rank ψ(s) is UPPER semicontinuous. Statement (iii)'s ordering for constants is just e ≤ e′; closure of stratum e hits only strata e′ ≥ e (rank jumps UP on closed subsets).
- **Boundedness / finitely many strata:** upper semicontinuity alone doesn't give finiteness; you need noetherian (quasicompact) S: each local presentation bounds e on its V by the number of generators, finitely many V's cover S. (Alternatively: {rank ≥ k} is a descending chain of closed subsets, stabilizes to ∅ by noetherianness.)
- **"Flat" vs "locally free of rank e":** the local universal property Nitsure actually proves is the FIXED-RANK one (f*F locally free of rank e ⟺ f factors through V_e); the "flat" form in (ii) is for the coproduct ∐_e S_e and uses: coherent + flat over locally noetherian ⟺ locally free (finitely presented flat modules over local rings are free), plus rank of a locally free sheaf is locally constant ⇒ T splits into open-closed constant-rank pieces T_e. A morphism to ∐ S_e is exactly such a splitting plus factorizations T_e → S_e. Formalization must keep "locally free of rank e" (constant rank) and "locally free" (locally constant rank) distinct.
- **The nontrivial iff direction:** f*F locally free of rank e ⇒ f*ψ = 0 requires that a surjection O_T^e ↠ L onto a rank-e locally free L has zero kernel. Nitsure gives no proof (the whole iff is asserted from right-exactness). Standard argument: at each t ∈ T the fibre map κ(t)^e → L ⊗ κ(t) is a surjection of e-dimensional spaces hence bijective; so ker ⊗ κ(t) = 0 (using that L locally free makes the sequence 0 → K → O^e → L → 0 exact and remain exact after ⊗κ(t)); Nakayama + K finite type (kernel of map of coherents) gives K = 0 near t. Equivalently the determinant trick.
- **Two applications of Nakayama in Step 1:** first to lift a basis of F|_s to generators near s (needs F coherent), second to present the kernel (needs the kernel coherent — automatic over noetherian S). The minimality (exactly e generators) is what forces s ∈ V_e; with a non-minimal presentation the entry ideal would cut out the wrong (empty-at-s) subscheme.
- **Well-definedness / independence of the presentation:** the scheme structure on V_e a priori depends on ψ; independence and gluing both come for free from the universal property (representing object of the subfunctor T ↦ {f : f*F locally free of rank e} is unique up to unique isomorphism). This is why Nitsure can glue "by their universal property" (L1844–1846) and why S_f is "uniquely determined" (L1833–1834).
- **e = 0 stratum:** locally free of rank 0 = zero sheaf; V_0 is the open complement of supp F (Nakayama: F|_s = 0 ⇒ F = 0 near s), an OPEN subscheme — consistent with the entry-ideal picture (the presentation degenerates to F = 0 on V).
- **Related machinery already in the text:** Remark 3.6 (`vanishing scheme`, L1587–1596) gives the universal "closed subscheme where a homomorphism vanishes, through which any base change killing the hom factors" — the same mechanism as V_e = vanishing scheme of ψ; and the Exercise after Lemma 3.2 (L1424–1427) states the converse "F flat ⇒ π_*F(r) locally free for r ≫ 0". Exercise at L2097–2099 (flattening stratification of O_{S^red} on S) is a good sanity test case.

## 4. Scheme-gluing infrastructure in the project

- **Mathlib `Scheme.GlueData`** used at scale in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean`: `theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData` (1149) built from affine charts + transition isos + verified cocycle (`t'`-compat proofs at 888-1140); `Grassmannian := (theGlueData d r).glued` (1162-1166); overlap identification `pullbackιIso` via `(theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit` (1275-1284); `toSpecZ` + `ι_toSpecZ` (1293-1303). Complete worked example of "build a scheme by gluing"; file is sorry-free.
- **Mathlib morphism gluing**: `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms (𝒰 : OpenCover X) (f : ∀ x, 𝒰.X x ⟶ Y) (hf : compatible on pullbacks) : X ⟶ Y`, `Cover.hom_ext`, `ι_glueMorphisms` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Gluing.lean:439/451/462`); directed variant `glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` (`Cover/Directed.lean:216`).
- **Mathlib disjoint-union recognition** (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean`): `isOpenImmersion_sigmaDesc` (297): if all `α i : g i ⟶ X` are open immersions with pairwise disjoint ranges then `Sigma.desc α` is an open immersion; `nonempty_isColimit_cofanMk_of` (~312): covering + pairwise disjoint open subschemes make `S` the coproduct; `sigmaι_eq_iff` (191), `disjoint_opensRange_sigmaι` (202), `isEmpty_pullback_sigmaι_of_ne` (219) — these are exactly the tools for constructing/uniquifying `ψ : T ⟶ ∐ S_f` from a clopen decomposition of T.
- **Project module descent over a glue datum** — `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean` (sorry-free): `Scheme.Modules.glue (D : Scheme.GlueData) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules) (g : transition isos) (hC1) (hC2) : D.glued.Modules` (106) as equalizer of pushforwards; `glueLift` (176, equalizer.lift API); `glueProj` (1004), `glueRestrictionHom/glueRestrictEqualizerIso/glueRestrictProdIso` (1042-1064); bridge lemmas `glueData_bridge_src/mid/tgt` (70/79/91); `pullbackBaseChangeTransport` (57); `pullbackFreeIso (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I) : φ^*(free I) ≅ free I` (255); `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (291). `GrassmannianQuot.lean` (sorry-free) drives all of this to build the tautological quotient: `bundleTransition` (486), cocycle transport `bundleTransition_cocycle_transport` (1640).
- **Answer**: yes. For the target, gluing of local closed subschemes of one open `U_e` can be avoided entirely by gluing the IDEALS instead (a single `IdealSheafData` on `U_e`, with agreement on overlaps supplied by the local universal property / `ext_of_iSup_eq_top`); if scheme-level gluing were ever needed, `Scheme.GlueData` + the GrassmannianCells pattern and `Cover.glueMorphisms` are proven-out in-project. The disjoint coproduct `∐ S_f` and the glue-of-ψ over the rank-clopen cover of T come from `Limits.lean` sigma lemmas + `Cover.glueMorphisms`.

## 5. Flat + finitely presented ⇒ locally free / projective; clopen-by-rank

Mathlib (module level, all available at the pin):
- `Module.projective_of_finitePresentation [Flat R M] [FinitePresentation R M] : Projective R M` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/EquationalCriterion.lean:288`).
- `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing [IsLocalRing R] [Module.Finite R P] [Flat R P] : Free R P` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalRing/Module.lean:302`); also `free_of_flat_of_finrank_eq` (420, Stacks 02M9).
- `Module.FinitePresentation.of_finite_of_finitePresentation`-family (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Finiteness/ModuleFinitePresentation.lean:80`); over a Noetherian ring `Module.Finite ⇒ Module.FinitePresentation` is available (instance route) — relevant since `S` is noetherian, so section-finiteness (project's Stacks-01PC lemma) upgrades to fp of sections on S. Warning: `T` in the universal property is arbitrary, so on T one must transport fp of `φ^*F` sheaf-theoretically, or argue via factorization through the strata instead.
- **Rank/clopen infrastructure**: `Module.rankAtStalk` and `Module.isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk [Module.FinitePresentation R M] [Module.Flat R M] : IsLocallyConstant (rankAtStalk M)` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Spectrum/Prime/FreeLocus.lean:187/228`) — this is the exact engine for the clopen decomposition of `Spec R` (hence of affine pieces of T) by rank; plus the whole `Module.freeLocus` file. ## Suggested attack shape (synthesis of found parts)
1. Per rank e, define the open `U_e ⊆ S` (points where fibre rank ≤ e / Nakayama-generated by e sections) and the entry-ideal `I_e : IdealSheafData U_e` from local presentations (`QuasicoherentData` presentations give the ψ matrices; `ofIdeals`/`mkOfMemSupportIff` + `ext_of_iSup_eq_top` to globalize; universal property of the local `V_e`'s gives overlap agreement).
2. Stratum `S_e := I_e.subscheme`, `ι_e := I_e.subschemeι ≫ U_e.ι`; immersion by composition; disjointness/covering from rank set-theory.
3. Flatness over the stratum: `φ^*ψ = 0` on `V_e` makes `φ^*F` locally free of rank e (cokernel of zero map from finite free) — use `pullbackFreeIso`, `IsLocallyFreeOfRank`, then sections free ⇒ flat, packaged by `flat_section_of_affine_cover`.
4. Universal property: for `φ : T ⟶ S` with `CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) (φ^*F)`, decompose T into rank-clopens (`Module.isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk` on affine charts of T — sections of `φ^*F` are flat by hypothesis and fp;

import Mathlib

/-!
# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)

This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.

## Status (run 0010, T12 r5 — algebraic generic freeness CLOSED, axiom-clean)

**`genericFlatness` is now proved** (sorry-free), in the companion file

/-- The rank of `M` at the stalk of `p` is the rank of `Mₚ` as a `Rₚ`-module. -/
noncomputable
def rankAtStalk (p : PrimeSpectrum R) : ℕ :=
  Module.finrank (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) (LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl M)

lemma isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk_freeLocus [Module.FinitePresentation R M] :
    IsLocallyConstant (fun x : freeLocus R M ↦ rankAtStalk M x.1) := by
  refine (IsLocallyConstant.iff_exists_open _).mpr fun ⟨x, hx⟩ ↦ ?_
  have := Module.finrank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Rₚ p' l
  simp [Rₚ, rankAtStalk, this, hf'']

lemma isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk [Module.FinitePresentation R M] [Module.Flat R M] :
    IsLocallyConstant (rankAtStalk (R := R) M) := by
  let e : freeLocus R M ≃ₜ PrimeSpectrum R :=
    (Homeomorph.setCongr freeLocus_eq_univ).trans (Homeomorph.Set.univ (PrimeSpectrum R))
  convert! isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk_freeLocus.comp_continuous e.symm.continuous

@[simp]
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.asIdeal.primeCompl N) _ e |>.finrank_eq

/-- If `M` is `R`-free, its rank at stalks is constant and agrees with the `R`-rank of `M`. -/
@[simp]
lemma rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free [Module.Free R M] :
    rankAtStalk (R := R) M = Module.finrank R M := by
  ext p
  simp [rankAtStalk, finrank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free _ p.asIdeal.primeCompl
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.asIdeal.primeCompl M)]

lemma rankAtStalk_self [Nontrivial R] : rankAtStalk (R := R) R = 1 := by
  simp

/-- The rank of a module `M` at a prime `p` is equal to the dimension

lemma rankAtStalk_eq (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
    rankAtStalk M p = finrank p.asIdeal.ResidueField (p.asIdeal.Fiber M) := by
  let k := p.asIdeal.ResidueField
  let e : k ⊗[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal] (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal ⊗[R] M) ≃ₗ[k]
      k ⊗[R] M :=
    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _
  rw [← e.finrank_eq, finrank_baseChange, rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_tensorProduct]

/-- Variant of `Module.rankAtStalk_eq` for better rewriting. -/

    finrank p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M) = rankAtStalk M ⟨p, hp⟩ :=
  (rankAtStalk_eq ⟨p, hp⟩).symm

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Jingting Wang, Junyan Xu, Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.RingHom
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.TensorProduct
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Quotient
public import Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas

/-!
# The fiber of a ring homomorphism at a prime ideal

## Main results

* `Ideal.Fiber`: `p.Fiber S` is the fiber of a prime `p` of `R` in an `R`-algebra `S`,
  defined to be `κ(p) ⊗ S`.
* `PrimeSpectrum.preimageHomeomorphFiber` : We show that there is a homeomorphism between the
  fiber of the induced map `PrimeSpectrum S → PrimeSpectrum R` at a prime ideal `p` and
  the prime spectrum of `p.Fiber S`.
-/

@[expose] public section

open Algebra TensorProduct nonZeroDivisors

variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open IsLocalRing in
instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsLocalRing S] [IsLocalHom (algebraMap R S)] :
    IsLocalRing (ResidueField R ⊗[R] S) :=
  let eSp : ResidueField R ⊗[R] S ≃ₐ[R] S ⧸ (maximalIdeal R).map (algebraMap R S) :=
    (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).trans
      ((TensorProduct.quotIdealMapEquivTensorQuot S (maximalIdeal R)).symm.restrictScalars _)
  have : Nontrivial (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R ⊗[R] S) := by
    rw [eSp.nontrivial_congr, Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
    exact ((((local_hom_TFAE (algebraMap R S)).out 0 2 rfl rfl).mp inferInstance).trans_lt
      (inferInstance : (maximalIdeal S).IsMaximal).lt_top).ne
  .of_surjective' TensorProduct.includeRight.toRingHom
    (TensorProduct.mk_surjective _ _ _ residue_surjective)

namespace Ideal

lemma ResidueField.exists_smul_eq_tmul_one
    (x : S ⊗[R] p.ResidueField) : ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s, r • x = s ⊗ₜ[R] 1 := by
  obtain ⟨t, r, a, hrt, e⟩ := RingHom.SurjectiveOnStalks.exists_mul_eq_tmul
    p.surjectiveOnStalks_residueField x ⊥ isPrime_bot
  obtain ⟨t, rfl⟩ := IsLocalRing.residue_surjective t
  obtain ⟨⟨y, t⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective p.primeCompl t
  simp only [smul_def, Submodule.mem_bot, mul_eq_zero, algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero,
    IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff, not_or, IsLocalization.AtPrime.mk'_mem_maximal_iff] at hrt
  refine ⟨r * y, p.primeCompl.mul_mem hrt.1 hrt.2, y • a, ?_⟩
  rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight.commutes, smul_tmul,
    ← Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_apply]
  simpa [← tmul_smul, Submonoid.smul_def, ← smul_mul_assoc, smul_comm _ r,
    ← IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, ← algebraMap.coe_smul,
    ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply] using congr(t • $e)

/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.EssentialFiniteness
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.ResidueField.Basic
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.FractionRing
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.SurjectiveOnStalks

/-!
# The residue field of a prime ideal

We define `Ideal.ResidueField I` to be the residue field of the local ring `Localization.Prime I`,
and provide an `IsFractionRing (R ⧸ I) I.ResidueField` instance.

-/

@[expose] public section

open scoped nonZeroDivisors

variable {R S A B : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
variable [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B] (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime]

/--
The residue field at a prime ideal, defined to be the residue field of the local ring
`Localization.Prime I`.
We also provide an `IsFractionRing (R ⧸ I) I.ResidueField` instance.
-/
abbrev Ideal.ResidueField : Type _ :=
  IsLocalRing.ResidueField (Localization.AtPrime I)

/-- If `I = f⁻¹(J)`, then there is a canonical embedding `κ(I) ↪ κ(J)`. -/
noncomputable
abbrev Ideal.ResidueField.map (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal S) [J.IsPrime]
    (f : R →+* S) (hf : I = J.comap f) : I.ResidueField →+* J.ResidueField :=
  IsLocalRing.ResidueField.map (Localization.localRingHom I J f hf)

@[simp]
lemma Ideal.ResidueField.map_algebraMap (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal S) [J.IsPrime]
    (f : R →+* S) (hf : I = J.comap f) (r : R) :
    ResidueField.map I J f hf (algebraMap _ _ r) = algebraMap _ _ (f r) := by
  rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R (Localization.AtPrime I)]
  simp [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.map_residue, Localization.localRingHom_to_map]
  rfl

lemma RingHom.SurjectiveOnStalks.residueFieldMap_bijective
    {f : R →+* S} (H : f.SurjectiveOnStalks)
    (I : Ideal R) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal S) [J.IsPrime] (hf : I = J.comap f) :
    Function.Bijective (Ideal.ResidueField.map I J f hf) := by
  subst hf
  exact ⟨RingHom.injective _, Ideal.Quotient.lift_surjective_of_surjective _ _
    (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective.comp (H J ‹_›))⟩

/-- If `I = f⁻¹(J)`, then there is a canonical embedding `κ(I) ↪ κ(J)`. -/
noncomputable
def Ideal.ResidueField.mapₐ (I : Ideal A) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal B) [J.IsPrime]
    (f : A →ₐ[R] B) (hf : I = J.comap f.toRingHom) : I.ResidueField →ₐ[R] J.ResidueField where
  __ := Ideal.ResidueField.map I J f hf
  commutes' r := by
    simp [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R A I.ResidueField,
      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R B J.ResidueField]

@[simp] lemma Ideal.ResidueField.mapₐ_apply (I : Ideal A) [I.IsPrime] (J : Ideal B) [J.IsPrime]
    (f : A →ₐ[R] B) (hf : I = J.comap f.toRingHom) (x) :
    Ideal.ResidueField.mapₐ I J f hf x = Ideal.ResidueField.map I J _ hf x := rfl

variable {I} in
@[simp high] -- marked `high` to override the more general `FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_eq_zero_iff`
lemma Ideal.algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero {x} :
    algebraMap R I.ResidueField x = 0 ↔ x ∈ I := by
  rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R (Localization.AtPrime I),
    IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff]
  exact IsLocalization.AtPrime.to_map_mem_maximal_iff _ _ _

@[simp high] -- marked `high` to override the more general `FaithfulSMul.ker_algebraMap_eq_bot`
lemma Ideal.ker_algebraMap_residueField :

(piRight R S N M).symm (Pi.single i (x ⊗ₜ m)) = x ⊗ₜ Pi.single i m := by
  simp [piRight]

/-- Tensor product commutes with finite products on the left.
TODO: generalize to `S`-linear. -/
@[simp] def piLeft : (∀ i, M i) ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] ∀ i, M i ⊗[R] N :=
  TensorProduct.comm .. ≪≫ₗ piRight .. ≪≫ₗ .piCongrRight fun _ ↦ TensorProduct.comm ..

end

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Internal implementation detail: we should make this `private`. -/
def piScalarRightHomBil : N →ₗ[S] (ι → R) →ₗ[R] (ι → N) where
  toFun n := LinearMap.compLeft (toSpanSingleton R N n) ι
  map_add' x y := by
    ext i j
    simp
  map_smul' s x := by
    ext i j
    dsimp only [coe_comp, coe_single, Function.comp_apply, compLeft_apply, toSpanSingleton_apply,
      RingHom.id_apply, smul_apply, Pi.smul_apply]
    rw [← IsScalarTower.smul_assoc, _root_.Algebra.smul_def, mul_comm, mul_smul]
    simp

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
/-- For any `R`-module `N` and index type `ι`, there is a natural
linear map `N ⊗[R] (ι → R) →ₗ (ι → N)`. This map is an isomorphism if `ι` is finite. -/
def piScalarRightHom : N ⊗[R] (ι → R) →ₗ[S] (ι → N) :=
  AlgebraTensorModule.lift <| piScalarRightHomBil R S N ι

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
@[simp]
lemma piScalarRightHom_tmul (x : N) (f : ι → R) :
    piScalarRightHom R S N ι (x ⊗ₜ f) = (fun j ↦ f j • x) := by
  ext j
  simp [piScalarRightHom, piScalarRightHomBil]

variable [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]

/-- (Implementation): Inverse for `TensorProduct.piScalarRight`. -/
def piScalarRightInv : (ι → N) →ₗ[S] N ⊗[R] (ι → R) :=
  LinearMap.lsum S (fun _ ↦ N) S <| fun i ↦ {
    toFun := fun n ↦ n ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1
    map_add' := fun x y ↦ by simp [add_tmul]
    map_smul' := fun _ _ ↦ rfl
  }

@[simp]
private lemma piScalarRightInv_single (x : N) (i : ι) :
    piScalarRightInv R S N ι (Pi.single i x) = x ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1 := by
  simp [piScalarRightInv, Pi.single_apply, TensorProduct.ite_tmul]

/-- For any `R`-module `N` and finite index type `ι`, `N ⊗[R] (ι → R)` is canonically
isomorphic to `ι → N`. -/
def piScalarRight : N ⊗[R] (ι → R) ≃ₗ[S] (ι → N) :=
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    (piScalarRightHom R S N ι)
    (piScalarRightInv R S N ι)
    (by ext i x j; simp [Pi.single_apply])
    (by ext x i; simp [Pi.single_apply_smul])

@[simp]
lemma piScalarRight_apply (x : N ⊗[R] (ι → R)) :
    piScalarRight R S N ι x = piScalarRightHom R S N ι x := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma piScalarRight_symm_single (x : N) (i : ι) :
    (piScalarRight R S N ι).symm (Pi.single i x) = x ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1 := by
  simp [piScalarRight]

-- See also `TensorProduct.piScalarRight_symm_algebraMap` in
-- `Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Pi.lean`.

/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib

/-!
# Entry ideals of matrix presentations (Nitsure §4, `n = 0` substrate)

This file is the commutative-algebra substrate for the *universal* `n = 0`
flattening stratification (`flatLocusStratification_universal` in
`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`), following the special
case `n = 0` of the proof of the flattening-stratification theorem in
[Nitsure, §4].  Mathlib v4.31 has no Fitting-ideal vocabulary; for a module
presented by `e` generators the *entry ideal* of the relation matrix — the
ideal generated by all matrix entries, i.e. the ideal of `1 × 1` minors,
which for an `e`-generator presentation is the Fitting ideal
`Fitt_{e-1}` — substitutes for it.

## Main definitions

* `Module.MatrixPresentation R M e m`: a presentation of the `R`-module `M`
  by `e` generators and `m` relations, recorded as a relation matrix
  `ψ : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R` together with a surjection
  `π : R^e → M` with `ker π = range ψ`.
* `Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal`: the ideal generated by the entries
  of the relation matrix.
* `Ideal.fiberRank`: the dimension `dim_{κ(p)} (κ(p) ⊗[R] M)` of the fiber
  of `M` at a prime `p`.

## Main results

* `Module.MatrixPresentation.baseChange`: a presentation base-changes along
  any `R`-algebra `A` (right-exactness of the tensor product), with
  `entryIdeal_baseChange`: the entry ideal of the base change is the image
  ideal.
* `Module.MatrixPresentation.projEquiv`: if the relation matrix vanishes,
  the presentation exhibits `M` as free of rank `e`
  (`rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero`).
* `Module.MatrixPresentation.relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat`: **the hard
  direction of Nitsure's local universal property** — if `M` is flat with
  `rankAtStalk M ≡ e`, the relation matrix of *any* `e`-generator
  presentation vanishes.  (The surjection `R^e ↠ M` between finite
  projectives of the same everywhere-rank is bijective.)
* `Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_eq`: presentation-independence of
  the entry ideal (the Fitting-ideal miracle for `1 × 1` minors), by
  base-changing each presentation to `R ⧸ entryIdeal` of the other.
* `Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_le_prime_iff`: for a prime `p`,
  `entryIdeal ≤ p ↔ fiberRank p M = e` — the point-set description of the
  rank-`e` stratum.
* `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule`:
  **Nakayama prolongation** — for `M` finitely presented and a prime `p`,
  there is `g ∉ p` such that every localization of `M` away from `g`
  admits a presentation with exactly `fiberRank p M` generators.

Blueprint chapter: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`, §entry-ideal.
Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
special case `n = 0` (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/`
`nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L1849–L1885).
-/

universe u v u' v'

open TensorProduct

namespace Module

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

/-- A **matrix presentation** of the `R`-module `M` by `e` generators and
`m` relations: a relation matrix `ψ : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R` and a
surjection `π : R^e → M` whose kernel is the column span of `ψ`, i.e.
`R^m —ψ→ R^e —π→ M → 0` is exact.  [Nitsure §4, n = 0 case: the local
presentation `𝒪_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝒪_V^{⊕e} → F|_V → 0`.] -/
structure MatrixPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
    (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (e m : ℕ) where
  /-- The relation matrix `ψ`. -/
  relMatrix : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R
  /-- The projection `R^e → M` picking out the `e` generators. -/
  proj : (Fin e → R) →ₗ[R] M
  surjective_proj : Function.Surjective proj
  exact_mulVecLin_proj : Function.Exact relMatrix.mulVecLin proj

namespace MatrixPresentation

variable {e m m' : ℕ}

lemma ker_proj (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
    LinearMap.ker P.proj = LinearMap.range P.relMatrix.mulVecLin :=
  LinearMap.exact_iff.mp P.exact_mulVecLin_proj

/-- A module admitting a matrix presentation is finitely presented. -/
lemma finitePresentation (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
    Module.FinitePresentation R M := by
  refine Module.finitePresentation_of_surjective P.proj P.surjective_proj ?_
  rw [P.ker_proj, LinearMap.range_eq_map]
  exact Module.Finite.fg_top.map _

/-- The **entry ideal** of a matrix presentation: the ideal generated by all
entries of the relation matrix (the ideal of `1 × 1` minors; for an
`e`-generator presentation this is the Fitting ideal `Fitt_{e-1} M`).
[Nitsure §4: "the ideal sheaf generated by the entries `ψ_{i,j}`".] -/
def entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) : Ideal R :=
  Ideal.span (Set.range fun ij : Fin e × Fin m ↦ P.relMatrix ij.1 ij.2)

lemma entryIdeal_le_iff {P : MatrixPresentation R M e m} {I : Ideal R} :
    P.entryIdeal ≤ I ↔ ∀ i j, P.relMatrix i j ∈ I := by
  rw [entryIdeal, Ideal.span_le]
  constructor
  · intro h i j
    exact h ⟨(i, j), rfl⟩
  · rintro h _ ⟨⟨i, j⟩, rfl⟩
    exact h i j

lemma relMatrix_mem_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (i : Fin e) (j : Fin m) : P.relMatrix i j ∈ P.entryIdeal :=
  entryIdeal_le_iff.mp le_rfl i j

section BaseChange

variable (A : Type u') [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]

/-- Vanishing of the base-changed relation matrix is containment of the
entry ideal in the kernel: `ψ ⊗ A = 0 ↔ I(ψ) ⊆ ker (R → A)`. -/
lemma relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff {P : MatrixPresentation R M e m} :
    P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A) = 0 ↔
      P.entryIdeal ≤ RingHom.ker (algebraMap R A) := by
  rw [entryIdeal_le_iff, ← Matrix.ext_iff]
  simp [RingHom.mem_ker]

/-- Base change intertwines `mulVecLin` of the mapped matrix with the
base-changed `mulVecLin`, through the scalar-extension isomorphisms
`A ⊗[R] (ι → R) ≃ (ι → A)`. -/
lemma mulVecLin_map_algebraMap (ψ : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin m) R) :
    (ψ.map (algebraMap R A)).mulVecLin =
      (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).toLinearMap
        ∘ₗ ψ.mulVecLin.baseChange A
        ∘ₗ (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m)).symm.toLinearMap := by
  refine LinearMap.pi_ext' fun j ↦ LinearMap.ext_ring ?_
  ext i
  simp [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec_single_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one,
    Matrix.mulVec_eq_sum]

/-- The base change of a matrix presentation along an `R`-algebra `A`:
`A^m —ψ⊗A→ A^e —π⊗A→ A ⊗[R] M → 0` (right-exactness of the tensor
product).  [Nitsure §4: "the pulled-back sequence is exact by
right-exactness of tensor products".] -/
noncomputable def baseChange (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
    MatrixPresentation A (A ⊗[R] M) e m where
  relMatrix := P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A)
  proj := P.proj.baseChange A ∘ₗ
    (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm.toLinearMap
  surjective_proj := by
    have h1 : Function.Surjective (P.proj.baseChange A) := by
      rw [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
      exact LinearMap.lTensor_surjective A P.surjective_proj
    exact h1.comp (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm.surjective
  exact_mulVecLin_proj := by
    rw [mulVecLin_map_algebraMap]
    have hex : Function.Exact (P.relMatrix.mulVecLin.baseChange A)
        (P.proj.baseChange A) := by
      simp only [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
      exact lTensor_exact A P.exact_mulVecLin_proj P.surjective_proj
    intro y
    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap]
    rw [hex ((TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).symm y)]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨x, hx⟩
      exact ⟨TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m) x, by simp [hx]⟩
    · rintro ⟨x, hx⟩
      refine ⟨(TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin m)).symm x, ?_⟩
      apply_fun (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)) using
        (TensorProduct.piScalarRight R A A (Fin e)).injective
      simpa using hx

@[simp]
lemma baseChange_relMatrix (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
    (P.baseChange A).relMatrix = P.relMatrix.map (algebraMap R A) := rfl

/-- The entry ideal of the base-changed presentation is the image of the
entry ideal. -/
lemma entryIdeal_baseChange (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) :
    (P.baseChange A).entryIdeal = P.entryIdeal.map (algebraMap R A) := by
  rw [entryIdeal, entryIdeal, Ideal.map_span]
  congr 1
  ext x
  simp [Matrix.map_apply, eq_comm]

end BaseChange

section Vanishing

/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, the projection of the presentation is
an isomorphism `R^e ≃ M`. -/
noncomputable def projEquiv (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) : (Fin e → R) ≃ₗ[R] M :=
  LinearEquiv.ofBijective P.proj
    ⟨by
      rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, P.ker_proj, h]
      simp, P.surjective_proj⟩

@[simp]
lemma projEquiv_apply (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (h : P.relMatrix = 0)
    (x : Fin e → R) : P.projEquiv h x = P.proj x := rfl

/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, `M` is free (of rank `e`). -/
lemma free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) : Module.Free R M :=
  Module.Free.of_equiv (P.projEquiv h)

/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, `M` has rank `e` at every prime. -/
lemma rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
    Module.rankAtStalk M p = e := by
  haveI : Nontrivial R := by
    refine ⟨0, 1, fun h01 ↦ p.isPrime.ne_top ?_⟩
    rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
    exact h01 ▸ p.asIdeal.zero_mem
  rw [← Module.rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv (P.projEquiv h),
    Module.rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free]
  simp

end Vanishing

section HardDirection

/-- **The hard direction of Nitsure's local universal property** (the step
left implicit in [Nitsure §4]): if `M` is flat and has rank `e` at every
prime, then the relation matrix of *any* presentation of `M` by `e`
generators vanishes.

The surjection `π : R^e ↠ M` between finite projectives of the same
everywhere-rank splits, the complement `K = ker π` is a finite projective
of everywhere-rank `0`, hence zero, so `ker π = range ψ = 0` and all
entries of `ψ` vanish. -/
theorem relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    [Module.Flat R M]
    (h : ∀ p : PrimeSpectrum R, Module.rankAtStalk M p = e) :
    P.relMatrix = 0 := by
  haveI := P.finitePresentation
  haveI : Module.Projective R M := Module.Flat.projective_of_finitePresentation
  -- split the projection
  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property P.proj LinearMap.id
    P.surjective_proj
  have hs' : ∀ x : M, P.proj (s x) = x := fun x ↦ congr($hs x)
  -- the kernel as a module
  set K := LinearMap.ker P.proj with hK
  -- `R^e ≃ M × K` via the splitting
  let E : (Fin e → R) ≃ₗ[R] M × K :=
    { toFun := fun x ↦ (P.proj x, ⟨x - s (P.proj x), by simp [hs']⟩)
      map_add' := fun x y ↦ by
        ext <;> simp [add_sub_add_comm]
      map_smul' := fun r x ↦ by
        ext <;> simp [smul_sub]
      invFun := fun nk ↦ s nk.1 + nk.2.1
      left_inv := fun x ↦ by simp
      right_inv := fun nk ↦ by
        have hk : P.proj nk.2.1 = 0 := nk.2.2
        ext <;> simp [hs', hk] }
  -- `K` is finite projective (a direct summand of `R^e`)
  haveI : Module.Projective R K := by
    refine Module.Projective.of_split (K.subtype) ((LinearMap.snd R M K) ∘ₗ E.toLinearMap) ?_
    ext ⟨x, hx⟩
    have : P.proj x = 0 := hx
    simp [E, this]
  haveI : Module.Finite R K := by
    have hsurj : Function.Surjective ((LinearMap.snd R M K) ∘ₗ E.toLinearMap) := by
      intro k
      exact ⟨E.symm (0, k), by simp⟩
    exact Module.Finite.of_surjective _ hsurj
  -- rank bookkeeping: `e = e + rankAtStalk K p` at every prime
  have hrank : ∀ p : PrimeSpectrum R, Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) K p = 0 := by
    intro p
    haveI : Nontrivial R := by
      refine ⟨0, 1, fun h01 ↦ p.isPrime.ne_top ?_⟩
      rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
      exact h01 ▸ p.asIdeal.zero_mem
    have h1 : Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (Fin e → R) p = e := by
      rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free]
      simp
    rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv E, Module.rankAtStalk_prod] at h1
    have h2 := h p
    omega
  -- everywhere-rank-zero finite flat module is zero
  haveI : Subsingleton K := by
    rw [← Module.rankAtStalk_eq_zero_iff_subsingleton]
    ext p
    exact hrank p
  -- hence the kernel is trivial and the relation matrix vanishes
  have hker : LinearMap.ker P.proj = ⊥ := by
    rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff]
    intro x hx
    exact congrArg Subtype.val (Subsingleton.elim (⟨x, hx⟩ : K) ⟨0, K.zero_mem⟩)
  have hrange : LinearMap.range P.relMatrix.mulVecLin = ⊥ := by
    rw [← P.ker_proj]
    exact hker
  rw [LinearMap.range_eq_bot] at hrange
  ext i j
  have := congr($hrange (Pi.single j 1) i)
  simpa [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec_single_one] using this

end HardDirection

section Independence

/-- Auxiliary half of presentation-independence: the entry ideal of one
`e`-generator presentation is contained in that of another. -/
theorem entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (Q : MatrixPresentation R M e m') :
    Q.entryIdeal ≤ P.entryIdeal := by
  -- base change both to `A := R ⧸ P.entryIdeal`
  set A := R ⧸ P.entryIdeal
  -- the base change of `P` has vanishing relation matrix
  have hP : (P.baseChange A).relMatrix = 0 := by
    rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff]
    intro x hx
    rw [RingHom.mem_ker, Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
    exact hx
  -- hence `A ⊗ M` is free of everywhere-rank `e`
  haveI : Module.Free A (A ⊗[R] M) := (P.baseChange A).free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero hP
  -- apply the hard direction to the base change of `Q`
  have hQ : (Q.baseChange A).relMatrix = 0 :=
    (Q.baseChange A).relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat
      ((P.baseChange A).rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero hP)
  rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff] at hQ
  refine hQ.trans ?_
  intro x hx
  rwa [RingHom.mem_ker, Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq,
    Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] at hx

/-- **Presentation-independence of the entry ideal** (the Fitting-ideal
miracle for `1 × 1` minors of an `e`-generator presentation): any two
presentations of `M` by `e` generators have the same entry ideal.
[Nitsure §4: implicit in "the resulting strata glue together by their
universal property".] -/
theorem entryIdeal_eq (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (Q : MatrixPresentation R M e m') :
    P.entryIdeal = Q.entryIdeal :=
  le_antisymm (entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal Q P) (entryIdeal_le_entryIdeal P Q)

end Independence

end MatrixPresentation

end Module

/-! ## The fiber rank and the point-set description of the strata -/

namespace Ideal

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R]

/-- The **fiber rank** of a module `M` at a prime `p`: the dimension of the
fiber `κ(p) ⊗[R] M` over the residue field `κ(p)`.  [Nitsure §4:
`e = dim_{κ(s)} F|_s`.] -/
noncomputable def fiberRank (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
    (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] : ℕ :=
  Module.finrank p.ResidueField (p.Fiber M)

end Ideal

namespace Module.MatrixPresentation

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
variable {e m : ℕ}

/-- **Point-set description of the rank-`e` stratum** [Nitsure §4, part (i)
of the theorem, `n = 0` case]: a prime `p` contains the entry ideal of an
`e`-generator presentation iff the fiber of `M` at `p` has dimension
exactly `e`. -/
theorem entryIdeal_le_prime_iff (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
    P.entryIdeal ≤ p ↔ p.fiberRank M = e := by
  have hker : RingHom.ker (algebraMap R p.ResidueField) = p :=
    Ideal.ker_algebraMap_residueField p
  constructor
  · -- entries in `p` ⟹ the fiber presentation has zero relation matrix
    intro hle
    have h0 : (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix = 0 := by
      rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff, hker]
      exact hle
    rw [Ideal.fiberRank, ← LinearEquiv.finrank_eq
      ((P.baseChange p.ResidueField).projEquiv h0)]
    simp
  · -- fiber dimension `e` ⟹ the fiber relation matrix vanishes (hard
    -- direction over the residue field), so the entries lie in `p`
    intro hrank
    have h0 : (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix = 0 := by
      refine (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat ?_
      intro q
      rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free]
      exact hrank
    rw [baseChange_relMatrix, relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff, hker] at h0
    exact h0

end Module.MatrixPresentation

/-! ## Nakayama prolongation

For `M` finitely presented over `R` and a prime `p`, there is `g ∉ p` such
that every localization of `M` away from `g` admits a matrix presentation
with exactly `fiberRank p M` generators.  This is the "prolongation of a
fiber basis" step of [Nitsure §4] (Nakayama applied twice: once to lift a
fiber basis to local generators, once to finitely present the kernel). -/

namespace Module.FinitePresentation

open Module

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

/-- **Nakayama prolongation of a fiber basis** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0` case,
first step]: for `M` finitely presented over `R` and `p` a prime, there is
`g ∉ p` such that every localization `(R', N)` of `(R, M)` away from `g`
admits a matrix presentation with exactly `e = fiberRank p M` generators.

The statement quantifies over abstract localizations (`IsLocalization.Away`
/ `IsLocalizedModule`) so that geometric section rings `Γ(S, D(g))` and
section modules `Γ(F, D(g))` can be consumed directly. -/
theorem exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule
    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
    ∃ g : R, g ∉ p ∧
      ∀ (R' : Type u') (_ : CommRing R') (_ : Algebra R R'),
        IsLocalization.Away g R' →
      ∀ (N : Type v') (_ : AddCommGroup N) (_ : Module R N) (_ : Module R' N)
        (_ : IsScalarTower R R' N) (l : M →ₗ[R] N),
        IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) l →
      ∃ mm : ℕ, Nonempty (MatrixPresentation R' N (p.fiberRank M) mm) := by
  classical
  set e := p.fiberRank M with he
  set κ := p.ResidueField with hκ
  -- a basis of the fiber, rescaled to consist of pure tensors `1 ⊗ mᵢ`
  haveI : Module.Finite κ (p.Fiber M) := inferInstance
  let b : Basis (Fin e) κ (p.Fiber M) := Module.finBasis κ (p.Fiber M)
  have hb : ∀ i : Fin e, ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • b i = (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] s := fun i ↦
    Ideal.Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul p (b i)
  choose r hr mvec hm using hb
  -- the images `1 ⊗ mᵢ` span the fiber
  have hspan : Submodule.span κ (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) = ⊤ := by
    have hunit : ∀ i, IsUnit (algebraMap R κ (r i)) := by
      intro i
      rw [isUnit_iff_ne_zero, Ne, Ideal.algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero]
      exact hr i
    have hsm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i = (hunit i).unit • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [← hm i]
      simp [Algebra.smul_def, algebraMap_smul]
    rw [show (fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) = fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit • b i from
      funext hsm]
    exact (b.unitsSMul hunit.unit... sorry : Submodule.span κ _ = ⊤)
  sorry

end Module.FinitePresentation

theorem exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule
    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
    ∃ g : R, g ∉ p ∧
      ∀ (R' : Type u') [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R'] [IsLocalization.Away g R']
        (N : Type v') [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module R' N]
        [IsScalarTower R R' N] (l : M →ₗ[R] N)
        [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) l],
      ∃ mm : ℕ, Nonempty (MatrixPresentation R' N (p.fiberRank M) mm) := by
  classical
  set e := p.fiberRank M with he
  set κ := p.ResidueField with hκ
  -- a basis of the fiber, rescaled to consist of pure tensors `1 ⊗ mᵢ`
  let b : Basis (Fin e) κ (p.Fiber M) := Module.finBasis κ (p.Fiber M)
  have hb : ∀ i : Fin e, ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • b i = (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] s := fun i ↦
    Ideal.Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul p (b i)
  choose r hr mvec hm using hb
  -- the images `1 ⊗ mᵢ` span the fiber
  have hunit : ∀ i, IsUnit (algebraMap R κ (r i)) := by
    intro i
    rw [isUnit_iff_ne_zero, Ne, Ideal.algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero]
    exact hr i
  have hspan : Submodule.span κ (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) = ⊤ := by
    have hsm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i = (hunit i).unit • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [← hm i, ← algebraMap_smul κ (r i) (b i), Units.smul_def, IsUnit.unit_spec]
    rw [show (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) =
        Set.range (b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit) from by
      simp only [funext hsm]
      rfl]
    exact (b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit).span_eq
  -- the `mᵢ` generate `M` modulo Nakayama: the quotient has vanishing fiber
  set α := Fintype.linearCombination R mvec with hα
  set N₀ := LinearMap.range α with hN₀
  have hQfiber : Subsingleton (κ ⊗[R] (M ⧸ N₀)) := by
    have hsurj : Function.Surjective (N₀.mkQ.baseChange κ) := by
      rw [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
      exact LinearMap.lTensor_surjective κ (Submodule.mkQ_surjective N₀)
    have hcomp : (N₀.mkQ.baseChange κ) ∘ₗ (α.baseChange κ) = 0 := by
      rw [← LinearMap.baseChange_comp]
      have h0 : N₀.mkQ ∘ₗ α = 0 := by
        ext x
        simp [Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, hN₀]
      rw [h0, LinearMap.baseChange_zero]
    have hrange : LinearMap.range (α.baseChange κ) = ⊤ := by
      rw [← top_le_iff, ← hspan, Submodule.span_le]
      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
      exact ⟨(1 : κ) ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1, by
        simp [LinearMap.baseChange_tmul, hα, Fintype.linearCombination_apply_single]⟩
    refine subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 fun x ↦ ?_
    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := hsurj x
    have hy : y ∈ LinearMap.range (α.baseChange κ) := hrange ▸ Submodule.mem_top
    obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := hy
    exact congr($hcomp a)
  -- Nakayama at `p`: the finite quotient localizes to zero at `p`
  set Q := M ⧸ N₀ with hQ
  set Rp := Localization.AtPrime p with hRp
  set Qp := LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Q with hQp
  have e2 : Rp ⊗[R] Q ≃ₗ[Rp] Qp :=
    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange p.primeCompl Rp
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl Q)).equiv
  haveI : Module.Finite Rp Qp := Module.Finite.equiv e2
  have hQpsub : Subsingleton Qp := by
    have Echain : (κ ⊗[Rp] Qp) ≃ (κ ⊗[R] Q) :=
      ((TensorProduct.congr (LinearEquiv.refl Rp κ) e2.symm).toEquiv).trans
        (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R Rp κ κ Q).toEquiv
    haveI : Subsingleton (κ ⊗[Rp] Qp) := Equiv.subsingleton Echain
    exact (IsLocalRing.subsingleton_tensorProduct (R := Rp)).mp ‹_›
  -- extract `g ∉ p` annihilating the quotient
  obtain ⟨g, hgann, hgp⟩ : ∃ g ∈ Module.annihilator R Q, g ∉ p := by
    have hnmem : (⟨p, ‹_›⟩ : PrimeSpectrum R) ∉ Module.support R Q := by
      rw [Module.mem_support_iff]
      exact fun h ↦ (not_nontrivial_iff_subsingleton.mpr hQpsub) h
    rw [Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite] at hnmem
    exact SetLike.not_le_iff_exists.mp hnmem
  have hg : ∀ x : M, g • x ∈ N₀ := by
    intro x
    have := Module.mem_annihilator.mp hgann (Submodule.Quotient.mk (p := N₀) x)
    rwa [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero] at this
  refine ⟨g, hgp, ?_⟩
  intro R' _ _ _ N _ _ _ _ l _
  -- `N` is finitely presented over `R'`
  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation R' N :=
    Module.FinitePresentation.of_isBaseChange l
      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) R' l)
  -- the images `l mᵢ` generate `N` over `R'`
  set α' := Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i) with hα'
  have hgen : Function.Surjective α' := by
    rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, hα', Fintype.range_linearCombination,
      ← top_le_iff]
    intro n _
    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hxs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers g) l n
    have hs : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' (s : R)) := IsLocalization.map_units R' s
    have hgu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' g) :=
      IsLocalization.map_units R' ⟨g, Submonoid.mem_powers g⟩
    -- `l (g • x)` lies in the `R'`-span of the `l mᵢ`
    have hmem : l (g • x) ∈ Submodule.span R' (Set.range fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hg x
      have : l (g • x) = ∑ i, c i • l (mvec i) := by
        rw [← hc]
        simp [hα, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum]
      rw [this]
      exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun i _ ↦ Submodule.smul_of_tower_mem _ (c i)
        (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
    -- clear the units `s` and `g`
    have hn : algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g) • n = l (g • x) := by
      rw [map_mul, mul_smul, algebraMap_smul, algebraMap_smul, ← map_smul,
        smul_comm]
      congr 1
      rw [← hxs]
      rfl
    have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g)) := by
      rw [map_mul]
      exact hs.mul hgu
    have : n = hu.unit⁻¹ • l (g • x) := by
      rw [← hn, Units.smul_def, Units.smul_def, ← smul_assoc]
      simp [IsUnit.unit_spec, ← Algebra.smul_def]
    rw [this]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hmem
  -- the kernel is finitely generated; its generators are the relation matrix
  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.fg_ker α' hgen
  refine ⟨T.card, ⟨{
    relMatrix := Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin e → R') i
    proj := α'
    surjective_proj := hgen
    exact_mulVecLin_proj := ?_ }⟩⟩
  rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, ← hT]
  apply le_antisymm
  · -- each generator is a column of the matrix
    rw [Submodule.span_le]
    intro t ht
    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := T.equivFin.symm.surjective ⟨t, ht⟩
    refine ⟨Pi.single j 1, ?_⟩
    funext i
    simp [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec_single_one, hj]
  · -- every matrix-vector product is a combination of the columns
    rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
    have : (Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin e → R') i).mulVecLin x =
        ∑ j, x j • ((T.equivFin.symm j : Fin e → R')) := by
      funext i
      simp [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dotProduct,
        Finset.sum_apply, mul_comm]
    rw [this]
    exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun j _ ↦ Submodule.smul_mem _ _
      (Submodule.subset_span (T.equivFin.symm j).2)

end Module.FinitePresentation

/-- A ring with a prime ideal is nontrivial. -/
private lemma nontrivial_of_primeSpectrum (p : PrimeSpectrum R) : Nontrivial R :=
  ⟨0, 1, fun h01 ↦ p.isPrime.ne_top
    ((Ideal.eq_top_iff_one _).mpr (h01 ▸ p.asIdeal.zero_mem))⟩

/-- If the relation matrix vanishes, `M` has rank `e` at every prime. -/
lemma rankAtStalk_eq_of_relMatrix_eq_zero (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
    (h : P.relMatrix = 0) (p : PrimeSpectrum R) :
    Module.rankAtStalk M p = e := by
  haveI : Nontrivial R := nontrivial_of_primeSpectrum p
  calc Module.rankAtStalk M p
      = Module.rankAtStalk (R := R) (Fin e → R) p :=
        (congrFun (Module.rankAtStalk_eq_of_equiv (P.projEquiv h)) p).symm
    _ = e := by
        simpa [Module.finrank_pi] using
          congrFun (Module.rankAtStalk_eq_finrank_of_free (R := R) (M := Fin e → R)) p

end Vanishing

classical
  set e := p.fiberRank M with he
  set κ := p.ResidueField with hκ
  -- a basis of the fiber, rescaled to consist of pure tensors `1 ⊗ mᵢ`
  let b : Basis (Fin e) κ (p.Fiber M) := Module.finBasis κ (p.Fiber M)
  have hb : ∀ i : Fin e, ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • b i = (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] s := fun i ↦
    Ideal.Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul p (b i)
  choose r hr mvec hm using hb
  -- the images `1 ⊗ mᵢ` span the fiber
  have hunit : ∀ i, IsUnit (algebraMap R κ (r i)) := by
    intro i
    rw [isUnit_iff_ne_zero, Ne, Ideal.algebraMap_residueField_eq_zero]
    exact hr i
  have hspan : Submodule.span κ (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) = ⊤ := by
    have hsm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i = (hunit i).unit • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [← hm i, ← algebraMap_smul κ (r i) (b i), Units.smul_def, IsUnit.unit_spec]
    rw [show (Set.range fun i ↦ (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] mvec i) =
        Set.range (b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit) from by
      simp only [funext hsm]
      rfl]
    exact (b.unitsSMul fun i ↦ (hunit i).unit).span_eq
  -- the `mᵢ` generate `M` modulo Nakayama: the quotient has vanishing fiber
  set α := Fintype.linearCombination R mvec with hα
  set N₀ := LinearMap.range α with hN₀
  have hQfiber : Subsingleton (κ ⊗[R] (M ⧸ N₀)) := by
    have hsurj : Function.Surjective (N₀.mkQ.baseChange κ) := by
      rw [LinearMap.baseChange_eq_ltensor]
      exact LinearMap.lTensor_surjective κ (Submodule.mkQ_surjective N₀)
    have hcomp : (N₀.mkQ.baseChange κ) ∘ₗ (α.baseChange κ) = 0 := by
      rw [← LinearMap.baseChange_comp]
      have h0 : N₀.mkQ ∘ₗ α = 0 := by
        ext x
        simp [Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, hN₀]
      rw [h0, LinearMap.baseChange_zero]
    have hrange : LinearMap.range (α.baseChange κ) = ⊤ := by
      rw [← top_le_iff, ← hspan, Submodule.span_le]
      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
      exact ⟨(1 : κ) ⊗ₜ Pi.single i 1, by
        simp [LinearMap.baseChange_tmul, hα, Fintype.linearCombination_apply_single]⟩
    apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0
    intro x
    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := hsurj x
    have hy : y ∈ LinearMap.range (α.baseChange κ) := hrange ▸ Submodule.mem_top
    obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := hy
    exact congr($hcomp a)
  -- Nakayama at `p`: the finite quotient localizes to zero at `p`
  set Q := M ⧸ N₀ with hQ
  set Rp := Localization.AtPrime p with hRp
  set Qp := LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Q with hQp
  have e2 : Rp ⊗[R] Q ≃ₗ[Rp] Qp :=
    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange p.primeCompl Rp
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl Q)).equiv
  haveI : Module.Finite Rp Qp := Module.Finite.equiv e2
  have hQpsub : Subsingleton Qp := by
    have Echain : (κ ⊗[Rp] Qp) ≃ (κ ⊗[R] Q) :=
      ((TensorProduct.congr (LinearEquiv.refl Rp κ) e2.symm).toEquiv).trans
        (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R Rp κ κ Q).toEquiv
    haveI : Subsingleton (κ ⊗[Rp] Qp) := Equiv.subsingleton Echain
    exact (IsLocalRing.subsingleton_tensorProduct (R := Rp)).mp ‹_›
  -- extract `g ∉ p` annihilating the quotient
  obtain ⟨g, hgann, hgp⟩ : ∃ g ∈ Module.annihilator R Q, g ∉ p := by
    have hnmem : (⟨p, ‹_›⟩ : PrimeSpectrum R) ∉ Module.support R Q := by
      rw [Module.mem_support_iff]
      exact fun h ↦ (not_nontrivial_iff_subsingleton.mpr hQpsub) h
    rw [Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite] at hnmem
    exact SetLike.not_le_iff_exists.mp hnmem
  have hg : ∀ x : M, g • x ∈ N₀ := by
    intro x
    have := Module.mem_annihilator.mp hgann (Submodule.Quotient.mk (p := N₀) x)
    rwa [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero] at this
  refine ⟨g, hgp, ?_⟩
  intro R' _ _ _ N _ _ _ _ l _
  -- `N` is finitely presented over `R'`
  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation R' N :=
    Module.FinitePresentation.of_isBaseChange l
      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) R' l)
  -- the images `l mᵢ` generate `N` over `R'`
  set α' := Fintype.linearCombination R' fun i ↦ l (mvec i) with hα'
  have hgen : Function.Surjective α' := by
    rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, hα', Fintype.range_linearCombination,
      ← top_le_iff]
    intro n _
    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hxs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers g) l n
    have hs : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' (s : R)) := IsLocalization.map_units R' s
    have hgu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' g) :=
      IsLocalization.map_units R' ⟨g, Submonoid.mem_powers g⟩
    -- `l (g • x)` lies in the `R'`-span of the `l mᵢ`
    have hmem : l (g • x) ∈ Submodule.span R' (Set.range fun i ↦ l (mvec i)) := by
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hg x
      have : l (g • x) = ∑ i, c i • l (mvec i) := by
        rw [← hc]
        simp [hα, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum]
      rw [this]
      exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun i _ ↦ Submodule.smul_of_tower_mem _ (c i)
        (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
    -- clear the units `s` and `g`
    have hn : algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g) • n = l (g • x) := by
      rw [map_mul, mul_smul, algebraMap_smul, algebraMap_smul, ← map_smul,
        smul_comm]
      congr 1
      rw [← hxs]
      rfl
    have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R R' ((s : R) * g)) := by
      rw [map_mul]
      exact hs.mul hgu
    have : n = hu.unit⁻¹ • l (g • x) := by
      rw [← hn, Units.smul_def, Units.smul_def, ← smul_assoc]
      simp [IsUnit.unit_spec, ← Algebra.smul_def]
    rw [this]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hmem
  -- the kernel is finitely generated; its generators are the relation matrix
  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.fg_ker α' hgen

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

/-- **Nakayama prolongation of a fiber basis** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0` case,
input.new_string:
variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

/-- Every element of the fiber `κ(p) ⊗[R] M` is, up to a unit (an `r ∉ p`),
a pure tensor `1 ⊗ s`.  (The module version of
`Ideal.Fiber.exists_smul_eq_one_tmul`, which Mathlib states only for
algebras.) -/
private lemma exists_smul_eq_one_tmul (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
    (x : p.ResidueField ⊗[R] M) :
    ∃ r ∉ p, ∃ s : M, r • x = (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] s := by
  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero =>
    exact ⟨1, fun h ↦ p.isPrime.ne_top ((Ideal.eq_top_iff_one _).mpr h), 0, by simp⟩
  | tmul c m =>
    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := IsLocalRing.residue_surjective c
    obtain ⟨⟨a, t⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective p.primeCompl y
    refine ⟨t, t.2, a • m, ?_⟩
    have ht : (t : R) • (IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
          (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t))
        = algebraMap R p.ResidueField a := by
      rw [Algebra.smul_def,

        IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, ← map_mul, IsLocalization.mk'_spec',

    calc (t : R) • (IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
          (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t) ⊗ₜ[R] m)
        = ((t : R) • IsLocalRing.residue (Localization.AtPrime p)
            (IsLocalization.mk' (Localization.AtPrime p) a t)) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']

      _ = (a • (1 : p.ResidueField)) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by
          rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
      _ = (1 : p.ResidueField) ⊗ₜ[R] (a • m) := TensorProduct.smul_tmul a 1 m

      · rw [mul_smul, hsy, TensorProduct.tmul_smul]

variable (v : α → M)

/-- `Fintype.linearCombination R v f` is the linear combination of vectors in `v` with weights
in `f`. This variant of `Finsupp.linearCombination` is defined on fintype indexed vectors.

This map is linear in `v` if `R` is commutative, and always linear in `f`.
See note [bundled maps over different rings] for why separate `R` and `S` semirings are used.
-/
protected def Fintype.linearCombination : (α → R) →ₗ[R] M where
  toFun f := ∑ i, f i • v i
  map_add' f g := by simp_rw [← Finset.sum_add_distrib, ← add_smul]; rfl
  map_smul' r f := by simp_rw [Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]; rfl

theorem Fintype.linearCombination_apply (f) : Fintype.linearCombination R v f = ∑ i, f i • v i :=
  rfl

data.content:
---
name: blueprint-conventions
description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
---

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

20:\section{Setup and motivation}
47:\section{Coherent flatness over a base}
86:\section{Generic flatness}
97:\subsection{The dévissage layer}

645:\section{Existence of the flattening stratification}

1671:\section{Out of scope}
====TAIL====
  \item \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat} ---
    \texttt{IsFlat} as a morphism property and basic stability lemmas; this is
    flatness of the structure morphism, \emph{not} flatness of a quasi-coherent
    module over the base.
  \item \texttt{Module.FaithfullyFlat} and the descent infrastructure that
    consumes it.
\end{itemize}

\noindent What Mathlib does \emph{not} yet have, and that this chapter will
introduce in the new file
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}:
\begin{itemize}
  \item A predicate \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
    capturing \(S\)-flatness of a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type
    \(S\)-scheme (\cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}). This is the
    sheaf-level lift of \texttt{Module.Flat} via the affine-local
    characterization in the second paragraph of the definition.
  \item The constructive existence proof
    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}) from Nitsure~\S4, proved
    through \texttt{flatLocusReduction}
    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean}, the Noetherian-induction
    reduction via generic flatness) and packaged with injective labels by
    \texttt{flatLocusAssembly} (\cref{lem:flat_locus_assembly_lean}); the
    \(n = 0\) case \texttt{flatLocusStratification}
    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_stratification_lean}) is derived back from the
    existence theorem at \(\pi = \mathrm{id}_S\).
  \item The universal-property packaging
    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification\_universal}
    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}), bundling the \(n = 0\)
    stratification with the unique factorisation of morphisms with flat
    pullback through the stratum coproduct \(\coprod_f S_f \to S\); the
    projective-family upgrade needs \(\PP^n_S\) vocabulary and
    cohomology-and-base-change, and an eventual
    \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy}-style refinement remains
    future work.
  \item The dévissage layer of the algebraic generic-freeness lemma
    (\cref{sec:generic_freeness_devissage}) is formalized and proved
    (splicing, torsion base case, transport, prime-filtration assembly
    \texttt{GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}); the sole remaining
    algebraic gap is the normalisation core
    (\cref{lem:generically_free_domain_core}).
  \item The relative-curve specialisation
    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
    (\cref{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}) for the Route~A
    consumer.
\end{itemize}

Inputs from Mathlib that the proof consumes:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \texttt{CategoryTheory.Limits.Cofan} / \texttt{Sigma} for the coproduct
    \(\coprod_f S_f\).
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyClosedImmersion} (and the locally-closed
    subscheme API) for the strata \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\).
  \item \texttt{IsNoetherian} / \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Noetherian} for the
    Noetherian-induction termination argument.
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProjective} (or
    \texttt{ProjectiveMorphism}) and the relative-\(\PP^n\) construction, to
    state and unpack the projective-morphism hypothesis on
    \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\).
  \item Higher-direct-image base-change lemmas (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry
    .Cohomology}, or a Stacks-tag-02KH-shaped lemma; cf.\ the existing
    \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} use of the same tag) for the \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\)
    construction in \cref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}.
  \item Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity / Serre vanishing for the
    \texttt{N\_1}-existence step.  Mathlib does not yet have these in the
    relative form needed; a future sub-lemma may stub them or rely on
    \texttt{Module.IsTorsion} arguments on the graded module
    \(\bigoplus_{m \ge N} \pi_* \F(m)\).
\end{itemize}

\section{Out of scope}
\label{sec:flatstrat_out_of_scope}

This chapter packages \emph{only} the flattening stratification of a coherent
sheaf on a projective morphism, plus the relative-curve specialisation. The
following downstream / parallel constructions live in separate sibling
chapters and are explicitly out of scope here:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity} (Nitsure~\S2) --- input to the
    Quot construction, used implicitly inside the proof of
    \cref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata} as the assertion that the
    Hilbert function stabilises beyond a uniform \(N\).  Its own blueprint home
    is the planned chapter \texttt{Picard\_CastelnuovoMumford.tex} (not yet
    scaffolded).
  \item \textbf{Quot scheme construction} (Nitsure~\S5, A.2.b) --- consumer of
    the present chapter; its home is the planned \texttt{Picard\_QuotScheme.tex}.
  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor} (A.1.c) ---
    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex} (planned).
  \item \textbf{Picard scheme representability for a smooth proper curve}
    (A.2.c) --- the assembly theorem fed by both the Quot construction (which
    consumes this chapter) and the relative Picard functor; home is the
    planned \texttt{Picard\_FGAPicRepresentability.tex}.
\end{itemize}

data.content:
\section{Universal property of the strata}
\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}

\begin{theorem}

\leanok
[Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (case \(n = 0\))]
  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}
  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
  Then there exist a finite index set \(I\) and locally-closed immersions
  \(\iota_f : S_f \to S\) (\(f \in I\)) with pairwise disjoint images
  set-theoretically covering \(|S|\), such that the pullback of \(\F\)
  along \(\iota_f\) is flat over \(S_f\) for every \(f\) (in the sense of
  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}), and such that, writing
  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and \(i : S' \to S\) for the morphism
  assembled from the \(\iota_f\), the pair \((S', i)\) has the universal
  property: every morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) for which
  \(\varphi^* \F\) is flat over \(T\) factors \emph{uniquely} through
  \(i\).

  This is part (ii) of Nitsure's flattening-stratification theorem in the
  special case \(n = 0\), stated with flatness in place of local freeness of
  constant rank: for a finitely presented module the two notions agree
  locally, the rank decomposing \(T\) into clopen pieces.  Nitsure states an
  ``if and only if''; the converse direction --- any morphism factoring
  through \(i\) has flat pullback --- is stability of flatness under base
  change and is recorded separately once needed.  The universal property for
  a relative family (Nitsure's part (ii) for \(\F\) on \(\PP^n_S\), hence
  for projective \(\pi : X \to S\)) additionally requires the
  cohomology-and-base-change layer of \cref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
  and is not stated separately here; for a general \emph{proper} family the
  universal property is not asserted by any source in the reference library,
  and the reduced strata of \cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} do not
  witness it (over \(S = \Spec k[\varepsilon]\) with \(\F = \OO_S\) flat,
  the identity must factor through the stratification, forcing the canonical
  non-reduced stratum structure).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:flat_locus_open}
  By the case \(n = 0\) of the flattening stratification
  (\cref{lem:flat_locus_open}), with its canonical scheme structure: locally
  on \(S\), a basis of \(\F \otimes \kappa(s)\) prolongs by Nakayama to a
  right-exact presentation
  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\)
  on a neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\), and the closed subscheme
  \(V_e \subseteq V\) cut out by the ideal of matrix entries of \(\psi\)
  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\):
  for \(f : T \to V\) the pulled-back presentation stays right-exact, so
  \(f^*\F\) is locally free of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f^*\psi = 0\),
  if and only if \(f\) factors through \(V_e\).  The local strata glue by
  this representing property into locally-closed subschemes
  \(S_e \subseteq S\) with \(|S_e|\) the rank-\(e\) locus; the family is
  finite because the fibre rank of a coherent module is bounded on a
  noetherian scheme, and the images are disjoint and cover \(|S|\) since
  every point has a well-defined fibre rank.

  For the universal property, let \(\varphi : T \to S\) have
  \(\varphi^*\F\) flat over \(T\).  A finitely presented flat module is
  locally free, and its rank is locally constant, so \(T\) decomposes into
  clopen pieces \(T_e\) on which \(\varphi^*\F\) is locally free of
  constant rank \(e\); by the representing property each restriction
  \(\varphi|_{T_e}\) factors uniquely through \(S_e\), and the
  factorizations assemble to \(\varphi = i \circ \tilde\varphi\) with
  \(\tilde\varphi : T \to \coprod_e S_e\).  Uniqueness: the strata have
  disjoint images, so any factorization through \(i\) induces the same
  decomposition of \(T\) into clopen preimages, and on each piece the
  component through \(S_e\) is unique because \(\iota_e\) is an immersion,
  hence a monomorphism.
\end{proof}
